title: Exam 1 - Last Reminder
content:
Hi Class,

Here are a few things to remember for the exam tomorrow (and see the original announcement at the bottom of this message):

  • One note sheet (One-side Only) of Hand-Written notes. 
  • Bring a calculator that does not connect to the internet. 
  • Bring a pencil. 
  • Remember: 70 Minute Exam with Scantron. Pencils down at 70 minutes.
  • Fill in your scantron before time is up (do this as you take the exam).
    • Points will be deducted for missing: Name, Student ID or exam VERSION. 

Best Luck!

 

 

 

 

ORIGINAL Exam 1 — Announcement

Hello Class,

Exam: Wednesday, September 24th, 2025. During Class.

This is an in-class, paper exam. You must be in class to receive credit.

If you have accommodations, please plan to take your exam at the CU Testing Center.

Exam details are below. The scope is general and may not include all topics. Please study videos and class slides, in-class problems, and homework for full coverage.

Logistics

  • Time: 70 minutes (in class)
  • Format:
    • Section 1: Scantron multiple-choice
    • Sections 2 & 3: Handwritten problems (show work for partial credit)

What to bring / rules

  • Calculator required (any calculator). No phones.
  • One sheet of handwritten notes, single-sided only (no typed/printed notes).
  • No Excel / no computers.
  • Circle answers in the booklet and fill the Scantron. The scantron will drive your grade.
  • You may tear off the back page for scratch; put your name on it and turn it in with the exam.

Scope & emphasis (aligned with course materials)

  • Accounting cycle, journal entries, adjusting entries
  • Income statement (multi-step), discontinued operations (EPS labeling)
  • Balance sheet and retained earnings logic
  • Statement of cash flows (indirect, direct flows, classifications, etc)
  • Revenue recognition (ASC 606): bundles, allocation, variable consideration, etc

Important note about Cash Flows

  • There will not be a full Statement of Cash Flows problem like the large one we did in class.
  • The in-class SCF problem was more complex than the exam. Expect targeted SCF items, not a full build.

Practice

  • A practice exam with solutions will be posted soon. Use it to gauge timing and format. Questions are similar in style but not identical.

Tips

  • Master mechanics first (Dr/Cr effects, AJEs), then synthesize (I/S subtotals, EPS, SCF operating adjustments).
  • Bring a calculator and plan your time: multiple choice first, then written problems.

Questions? Email me.

You’ve got this!

Hi Class,
Here are a few things to remember for the exam tomorrow (and see the original announcement at the bottom of this message):

Bring a pencil.

ORIGINAL Exam 1 — Announcement

Hello Class,
Exam: Wednesday, September 24th, 2025. During Class.
This is an in-class, paper exam. You must be in class to receive credit.
If you have accommodations, please plan to take your exam at the CU Testing Center.
Exam details are below. The scope is general and may not include all topics. Please study videos and class slides, in-class problems, and homework for full coverage.
Logistics

Time: 70 minutes (in class)
Format:

Section 1: Scantron multiple-choice
Sections 2 & 3: Handwritten problems (show work for partial credit)


What to bring / rules

Calculator required (any calculator). No phones.
One sheet of handwritten notes, single-sided only (no typed/printed notes).
No Excel / no computers.
Circle answers in the booklet and fill the Scantron. The scantron will drive your grade.
You may tear off the back page for scratch; put your name on it and turn it in with the exam.

Scope & emphasis (aligned with course materials)

Accounting cycle, journal entries, adjusting entries
Income statement (multi-step), discontinued operations (EPS labeling)
Balance sheet and retained earnings logic
Statement of cash flows (indirect, direct flows, classifications, etc)
Revenue recognition (ASC 606): bundles, allocation, variable consideration, etc

Important note about Cash Flows

There will not be a full Statement of Cash Flows problem like the large one we did in class.
The in-class SCF problem was more complex than the exam. Expect targeted SCF items, not a full build.

Practice

A practice exam with solutions will be posted soon. Use it to gauge timing and format. Questions are similar in style but not identical.

Tips

Master mechanics first (Dr/Cr effects, AJEs), then synthesize (I/S subtotals, EPS, SCF operating adjustments).
Bring a calculator and plan your time: multiple choice first, then written problems.

Questions? Email me.
You’ve got this!
author: Jeremiah Contreras
